_comment_8: NOTE: JSON does not support real comments. These _comment fields are
_comment_9: for educational purposes. In your real config, you can remove them.

mcpServers._comment_server_1b: This is the most common pattern. Claude Desktop launches the Python
mcpServers._comment_server_1c: script as a subprocess and communicates via stdin/stdout.
mcpServers._comment_server_2b: Use the 'env' field to pass API keys or config to the server.
mcpServers._comment_server_2c: This keeps secrets out of your code.
mcpServers._comment_server_3b: MCP servers can be written in any language. Here is a Node.js example.
mcpServers._comment_server_3c: Many community servers are distributed as npm packages.
mcpServers._comment_server_4: --- EXAMPLE 4: Using uv to run a Python server ---
mcpServers._comment_server_4b: The 'uv' tool can run Python scripts with automatic dependency management.
mcpServers._comment_server_4c: This is convenient when the server needs specific packages.
mcpServers._comment_server_5b: You can run MCP servers inside Docker containers for isolation.
mcpServers._comment_server_6: --- EXAMPLE 6: Remote server via SSE (Server-Sent Events) ---
mcpServers._comment_server_6b: For remote/shared servers, you can use HTTP+SSE transport.
mcpServers._comment_server_6c: This lets multiple users share one MCP server running on a network.
